page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that this instance delivers modest performance. CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, fall into the lower performance tiers, with a single-core Geekbench score of 915.0 and a multi-core score of 1993.0. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read operations registering at 19.83 GB/sec and write operations at 18.72 GB/sec. These metrics suggest that while the instance offers a generous memory allocation, it is not designed for high-throughput computational tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.u0.c1m8.xlarge provides a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense CPU processing. However, the tradeoff is lower overall processing speeds and limited storage throughput. This profile makes the instance suitable for lightweight database caching, Redis key-value stores, and moderate memory-intensive background processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture via the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with modest CPU and memory bandwidth results, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 1993.0 and large-block memory read speeds of 19.83 GB/sec. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads with low compute demands. It is best suited for lightweight database caching, Redis operations, and memory-bound background tasks.
